prerequisites: A valid Spotify Ads Manager account (sign up at adsmanager.spotify.com).
A registered Spotify developer application with the "Ads API" selected, a configured redirect URI (for example http://127.0.0.1:8080/callback), and a client ID and client secret.
The Ads API Terms accepted for your client ID at adsmanager.spotify.com/api-terms (requests fail until this is done; allow up to one hour for allowlisting).
The ad_account_id of the Spotify Ads Manager ad account you want to report on, used as the path parameter on the reporting endpoints.

authMethods.name: OAuth 2.0 (authorization code flow with refresh token)
authMethods.summary: Spotify Ads API uses the OAuth 2.0 authorization code flow. Register an application in the Spotify developer dashboard to obtain a client_id and client_secret, then run the authorization flow once to grant your Ads Manager account access. Spotify returns an access token (valid about 1 hour) and a non-expiring refresh_token. The connector uses the refresh_token together with client_id and client_secret to mint fresh access tokens via POST to https://accounts.spotify.com/api/token with grant_type=refresh_token. Provide client_id, client_secret, and refresh_token as the connector secret fields.

setupSteps.title: Create your Spotify and Ads Manager accounts
setupSteps.body: Ensure you have a valid Spotify account and a valid Spotify Ads Manager account. Sign up for Ads Manager at https://adsmanager.spotify.com/signup/form if you do not already have one. The ad account you manage there is the source of the campaign and reporting data this connector reads.

setupSteps.title: Register an Ads API application
setupSteps.body: In the Spotify developer dashboard (developer.spotify.com), create an application to obtain a client ID and client secret. Configure a redirect URI for the application (for example http://127.0.0.1:8080/callback) and select "Ads API" among the APIs you plan to use. These values populate the connector's client_id and client_secret fields.

setupSteps.title: Accept the Ads API Terms for your client ID
setupSteps.body: Go to https://adsmanager.spotify.com/api-terms and accept the Ads API Terms using your client ID. This step is required: requests fail without it. Allow up to one hour for your client ID to be allowlisted before calling the API.

setupSteps.title: Authorize and obtain a refresh token
setupSteps.body: Run the OAuth authorization code flow once. Open https://accounts.spotify.com/authorize/?client_id=<CLIENT_ID>&response_type=code&redirect_uri=<REDIRECT_URI> in a browser, log in, and authorize the application. Take the code parameter from the redirect, then exchange it by POSTing to https://accounts.spotify.com/api/token (authenticating with your base64-encoded client_id:client_secret). The response contains an access_token (about 1 hour validity) and a non-expiring refresh_token. Provide that refresh_token to the connector.

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector fields
setupSteps.body: Set ad_account_id to the Ads Manager ad account you want to pull data for (it keys the reporting endpoints, e.g. "Get Aggregate Report by Ad Account ID"). Set fields to the metrics you want returned (metric names are defined in the Ads API Metrics Glossary). Set start_date to the beginning of the reporting window. Enter client_id, client_secret, and refresh_token as the credentials. The connector then refreshes access tokens automatically to read campaign and reporting data.

sources.title: Spotify Ads API - Quick Start (account prerequisites, app registration, OAuth, refresh token)
